pillars.text: We enable restorations where these aren't happening.

blockA.title: How It Works
blockA.description: Petra creates a platform for micro-mecenatism by directly connecting heritage buildings to visitors.

steps.title: Scan
steps.desc: the QR code you find around.

blockB.title: The Certificate
blockB.description: Every donation is certified and verifiable on a public distributed ledger.

cards.title: Unique
cards.desc: each certificate is derived from a hash proof associated with the donation.

cards.title: Verifiable
cards.desc: anyone can verify legitimacy by comparing the cryptographic proof against the public ledger.

cards.title: Permanent
cards.desc: once anchored on the ledger, the hash proofs cannot be altered or deleted.

blockC.title: Where Funds Go
blockC.description: A disintermediated micro-mecenatism model to maximize impact on restoration.

features.title: Directly
features.desc: to the responsible for restoration, in a transparent way.

features.title: Traceable
features.desc: impact is recorded and viewable through your certificates.

partners.title: Partners & Collaborators
partners.desc: Explore the possibility of collaborating with Petra or align your brand with cultural heritage preservation.

problem.title: In Art Cities
intro: Look up. The architecture that define these places — the reason millions of people cross the world — is falling apart. Not through negligence, but through a systemic failure.
Over-tourism, high costs of restoration, public benefit, no mechanism to directly internalize tourists willingness-to-pay.
This leads to the growing degradation of the architectural heritage that has an intrinsic very relevant historical-artistic value.
diagnosis: It is a textbook case of an uncompensated positive externality: those who benefit don't pay, those who pay don't benefit.

The owner pays. The tourist benefits. The owner stops paying. The tourist keeps coming, until there is nothing left to see. The result is chronic underinvestment in maintenance: not because owners don't care, but because the market fails to transfer to them the value their patrimony creates.

bottlenecks.title: Capital
bottlenecks.text: Restoring historic goods with artistic value is expensive. For the owners, it is a sunk cost: the value it generates — a more attractive urban landscape, a more livable city — is captured by the public, not by the one who pays. In the absence of compensation mechanisms, underinvestment is rational.

bottlenecks.title: Coordination
bottlenecks.text: Many of these goods have multiple owners, divergent interests, fragmented decision-making. Even when the will exists, there is no infrastructure to coordinate the intervention, aggregate resources, and manage the relationship with heritage authorities.

bottlenecks.title: Authorization
bottlenecks.text: Heritage protection regulations safeguard the patrimony, but in the absence of streamlined approval processes, they discourage even owners who want to act. The result is a system where restoring is harder than letting decay.

problem.note: Petra aims to heal the first pathology: capital. It does so with a mechanism that transfers micro-mecenatism value directly to those who steward the heritage. Coordination and authorization problems require different institutional reforms — but without resources, any reform would remain on paper.

about.title: About Us
about.intro: Petra was born from a simple question: why the people who visit art cities around the world every year don't contribute directly to the conservation of what they came to see?
about.mission: We are not a restoration association. We are not a crowdfunding platform. Petra is a protocol — a digital infrastructure that connects the tourists to the maintenance need. A QR code. A donation. A cryptographic certificate proving where every cent went. Our goal is to make contributing to beauty as natural as taking a photo.

team.name: Umberto Toraldo
team.bio: Economics student. He dedicated his bachelor's thesis to the ex-ante evaluation of crowdfunding as a financing tool for private architectural heritage in Venice. Petra is the operational translation of that research: an attempt to field-test a hypothesis grounded in academic research.

institutions.title: Become a Partner
institutions.intro: Petra offers local administrations and cultural institutions a digital infrastructure to mobilize tourist micro-donations towards the restoration of architectural heritage in their territory.

steps.title: Partnership
steps.desc: We sign a collaboration agreement. Petra provides the infrastructure, the institution identifies the construction sites.

steps.title: Operations
steps.desc: QR codes installed at construction sites. Donations go directly to your account via Stripe Connect.

steps.title: Data
steps.desc: You receive quarterly reports and tourist willingness-to-pay data, usable for your policy decisions.

institutions.cta: Write to us for an assessment

protocolBanner.title: Become a micro-patron
protocolBanner.desc: Discover how to donate, certify, and verify that funds contribute to restorations
